module-1-profile-card-project.label: Module Project: Build a Profile Card
module-1-profile-card-project.summary: A tutor wants one profile card program that grows from a simple stored value into a clean interactive card. Each step keeps the working code and upgrades it.

mp-1-store-profile-values.title: Build the first profile card
mp-1-store-profile-values.prompt:
Turn the preview into the first real card. Store `"Maya"` in `student_name`, store `7` in `level`, then print exactly:
Profile for Maya
Level: 7
mp-1-store-profile-values.hint: Store the values first, then print the two card lines.

help.concept: This step starts the actual project structure. The card now has labeled output instead of a single preview value.
help.hint_1: Use `student_name` for the name variable and `level` for the number.
help.hint_2: The first line starts with `Profile for`.

mp-1-store-profile-values.starterCode:
name = "Ava"
print(name)

# Replace the preview with a first profile card.
# Store Maya in student_name and 7 in level.
# Then print the two profile lines.

mp-2-read-and-label-input.title: Make the card read input
mp-2-read-and-label-input.prompt:
Keep the same card layout, but make it more useful. Replace the stored name with `input()`, keep `level = 7`, read the city with a second `input()`, and print exactly:
Profile for <name>
Level: 7
City: <city>
mp-2-read-and-label-input.hint: Use `input()` for the typed name and city, then keep printing the card in the same order.

help.concept: The project still feels like the same card, but now the learner can type values instead of relying only on stored text.
help.hint_1: The name comes from the first input and the city comes from the second input.
help.hint_2: Keep the level line in the middle.

mp-2-read-and-label-input.starterCode:
student_name = "Maya"
level = 7
print(f"Profile for {student_name}")
print(f"Level: {level}")

# Keep the profile card idea.
# Replace the stored name with input().
# Read the city with input() too, then add the city line.

mp-3-clean-typed-name.title: Clean the typed name
mp-3-clean-typed-name.prompt: Keep the same three-line card. This time, clean the typed name with `.strip()` and `.title()` before printing it.
mp-3-clean-typed-name.hint: Store the typed name first, then clean it before you print the card.

help.concept: This is an upgrade to the same program, not a reset. The card layout stays the same while the name handling gets better.
help.hint_1: Use one variable for the raw name and another for the cleaned result if that helps.
help.hint_2: Only the name needs cleaning in this step.

mp-3-clean-typed-name.starterCode:
name = input()
level = 7
city = input()
print(f"Profile for {name}")
print(f"Level: {level}")
print(f"City: {city}")

# Keep the card layout.
# Clean the typed name with .strip() and .title() before printing it.

mp-4-build-full-profile-card.title: Finish the full profile card
mp-4-build-full-profile-card.prompt:
Keep the cleaned-name card you already built. Now replace the level with a typed age, convert it to an integer, and print the finished card as:
Profile for <clean name>
Age next year: <age + 1>
City: <city>
mp-4-build-full-profile-card.hint: Keep the cleaned name logic, switch the middle value to `int(input())`, and print the updated middle line.

help.concept: The final step keeps most of the card structure the same, but makes the program more realistic by reading an age and calculating next year.
help.hint_1: The input order is name, age, city.
help.hint_2: The middle line should use `age + 1`, not the raw age.

mp-4-build-full-profile-card.starterCode:
raw_name = input()
level = 7
city = input()
name = raw_name.strip().title()
print(f"Profile for {name}")
print(f"Level: {level}")
print(f"City: {city}")

# Keep the cleaned name.
# Replace level with a typed age.
# Print Age next year instead of Level.

try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.title: Preview the profile card
try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.prompt: Store `"Ava"` in a variable named `name` and print it.
try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.hint: Assign first, then print the variable.

help.concept: This quick preview gives the project one clean stored value before the full profile card grows around it.
help.hint_2: Print the variable, not the text directly.

try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.starterCode:
# Store Ava in name.
# Then print name.

try_module_1_profile_card_project_sketch.title: Try it yourself
try_module_1_profile_card_project_sketch.prompt: Store `"Ava"` in a variable named `name` and print it.

sketch-1.title: Your first profile-card client
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A tutor is meeting several new students and wants one tiny profile card generator instead of rewriting the same information by hand every time.

This module project should feel like one connected build. You start with a very small card, then keep improving the same program until it reads input, cleans the name, and prints a polished final version.
